_meta.about: Curated method->citation seed for the nirs4all reproduction-document publisher.
_meta.note: Citation strings are authoritative; DOIs/URLs are present only where confidently known and should be enriched over time (e.g. via Crossref). A method without an entry renders without a citation and is flagged.

snv.label: Standard Normal Variate (SNV)
snv.authors: Barnes, R. J.; Dhanoa, M. S.; Lister, S. J.
snv.title: Standard Normal Variate Transformation and De-trending of Near-Infrared Diffuse Reflectance Spectra
snv.principle: Per-spectrum centring and scaling that removes multiplicative scatter and baseline offset row by row.

rnv.label: Robust Normal Variate (RNV)
rnv.authors: Guo, Q.; Wu, W.; Massart, D. L.
rnv.title: The robust normal variate transform for pattern recognition with near-infrared data
rnv.principle: A percentile-based variant of SNV that is resistant to outlying intensities.

msc.label: Multiplicative Scatter Correction (MSC)
msc.authors: Geladi, P.; MacDougall, D.; Martens, H.
msc.title: Linearization and Scatter-Correction for Near-Infrared Reflectance Spectra of Meat
msc.principle: Regresses each spectrum against a reference (mean) spectrum and corrects its slope/offset to remove scatter.

emsc.label: Extended Multiplicative Scatter Correction (EMSC)
emsc.title: Extended multiplicative signal correction and spectral interference subtraction
emsc.venue: Journal of Pharmaceutical and Biomedical Analysis 9(8), 625–635
emsc.principle: MSC augmented with polynomial and chemical-interference terms in the correction model.

savgol.label: Savitzky–Golay smoothing / derivative
savgol.authors: Savitzky, A.; Golay, M. J. E.
savgol.title: Smoothing and Differentiation of Data by Simplified Least Squares Procedures
savgol.principle: Local least-squares polynomial fitting in a moving window, giving smoothed values or analytic derivatives.

first_derivative.label: First derivative
first_derivative.authors: Norris, K. H.; Williams, P. C.
first_derivative.title: Optimization of mathematical treatments of raw near-infrared signal in the measurement of protein in hard red spring wheat
first_derivative.principle: Finite-difference / gap first derivative that removes additive baseline and sharpens overlapping bands.

second_derivative.label: Second derivative
second_derivative.authors: Norris, K. H.; Williams, P. C.
second_derivative.title: Optimization of mathematical treatments of raw near-infrared signal in the measurement of protein in hard red spring wheat
second_derivative.principle: Second-order finite-difference derivative that removes both additive and linear-multiplicative baselines.

detrend.label: De-trending
detrend.authors: Barnes, R. J.; Dhanoa, M. S.; Lister, S. J.
detrend.title: Standard Normal Variate Transformation and De-trending of Near-Infrared Diffuse Reflectance Spectra
detrend.principle: Removes a fitted low-order polynomial baseline from each spectrum.

gaussian_filter.label: Gaussian smoothing
gaussian_filter.title: Gaussian-kernel convolution smoothing
gaussian_filter.principle: Convolution with a Gaussian kernel to suppress high-frequency noise.

minmax.label: Min–max scaling
minmax.title: Scikit-learn: Machine Learning in Python (implementation reference)
minmax.principle: Affine rescaling of each feature to a fixed range (default [0, 1]); a standard feature-conditioning step.

standard_scaler.label: Standardisation (z-score)
standard_scaler.title: Scikit-learn: Machine Learning in Python (implementation reference)
standard_scaler.principle: Centres to zero mean and scales to unit variance; applied to features or to the regression target.

pls.label: Partial Least Squares regression (PLS)
pls.authors: Wold, S.; Sjöström, M.; Eriksson, L.
pls.title: PLS-regression: a basic tool of chemometrics
pls.principle: Projects spectra onto a small number of latent variables that maximise covariance with the target, then regresses.

simpls.label: SIMPLS
simpls.title: SIMPLS: an alternative approach to partial least squares regression
simpls.principle: Computes PLS factors directly from the covariance matrix without deflating the data block.

ikpls.label: Improved Kernel PLS (IKPLS)
ikpls.authors: Dayal, B. S.; MacGregor, J. F.
ikpls.title: Improved PLS algorithms
ikpls.principle: Kernel-form PLS algorithms that are fast when samples greatly outnumber or are outnumbered by features.

opls.label: Orthogonal PLS (OPLS)
opls.title: Orthogonal projections to latent structures (O-PLS)
opls.principle: Separates target-correlated variation from orthogonal (uncorrelated) variation before the PLS regression.

mbpls.label: Multi-block PLS (MB-PLS)
mbpls.authors: Westerhuis, J. A.; Kourti, T.; MacGregor, J. F.
mbpls.title: Analysis of multiblock and hierarchical PCA and PLS models
mbpls.principle: Fits PLS jointly over several data blocks, exposing per-block contributions.

kernel_pls_rbf.label: Kernel PLS (RBF)
kernel_pls_rbf.title: Kernel partial least squares regression in reproducing kernel Hilbert space
kernel_pls_rbf.principle: Non-linear PLS via the kernel trick in a reproducing-kernel Hilbert space.

pcr.label: Principal Component Regression (PCR)
pcr.title: Principal Components Regression in Exploratory Statistical Research
pcr.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association 60(309), 234–256
pcr.principle: Regresses the target on the leading principal components of the spectra.

pca.label: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
pca.title: Principal Component Analysis (2nd ed.)
pca.principle: Orthogonal projection onto directions of maximum variance; used here as a dimensionality-reduction transform.

ridge.label: Ridge regression
ridge.authors: Hoerl, A. E.; Kennard, R. W.
ridge.title: Ridge Regression: Biased Estimation for Nonorthogonal Problems
ridge.principle: L2-penalised linear regression that stabilises estimation on collinear spectral features.

kennard_stone.label: Kennard–Stone split
kennard_stone.authors: Kennard, R. W.; Stone, L. A.
kennard_stone.title: Computer Aided Design of Experiments
kennard_stone.principle: Greedily selects a calibration set that uniformly covers the feature space, leaving a representative test set.

spxy.label: SPXY split
spxy.authors: Galvão, R. K. H. et al.
spxy.title: A method for calibration and validation subset partitioning
spxy.principle: Kennard–Stone-style partitioning using joint distances in both X (spectra) and Y (target).

vip_select.label: Variable Importance in Projection (VIP)
vip_select.authors: Wold, S.; Sjöström, M.; Eriksson, L.
vip_select.title: PLS-regression: a basic tool of chemometrics
vip_select.principle: Ranks wavelengths by their contribution to the PLS projection for variable selection.

cars_select.label: Competitive Adaptive Reweighted Sampling (CARS)
cars_select.title: Key wavelengths screening using competitive adaptive reweighted sampling method for multivariate calibration
cars_select.principle: Iteratively reweights and resamples wavelengths to retain the most informative subset.

ipls_select.label: Interval PLS (iPLS)
ipls_select.title: Interval Partial Least-Squares Regression (iPLS): A Comparative Chemometric Study with an Example from Near-Infrared Spectroscopy
ipls_select.principle: Splits the spectrum into intervals and selects those giving the best local PLS model.

pds.label: Piecewise Direct Standardisation (PDS)
pds.authors: Wang, Y.; Veltkamp, D. J.; Kowalski, B. R.
pds.title: Multivariate instrument standardization
pds.principle: Calibration-transfer map between instruments built from local windows of standard spectra.

mixup.label: mixup augmentation
mixup.authors: Zhang, H.; Cisse, M.; Dauphin, Y. N.; Lopez-Paz, D.
mixup.title: mixup: Beyond Empirical Risk Minimization
mixup.principle: Trains on convex combinations of sample/label pairs to regularise the model.

aom_pls.label: AOM-PLS / POP-PLS
aom_pls.authors: Beurier, G.; Reiter, R.; Noûs, C.; Rouan, L.; Cornet, D.
aom_pls.title: Reframing preprocessing selection as model-internal calibration in NIRS
aom_pls.principle: Treats the choice of preprocessing as part of the model's internal calibration rather than a fixed pre-step.
